data.title: US banking industry prioritizes halting stablecoin yields in 2026 amid proposed crypto market regulations before midterms.
data.description: The American Bankers Association is currently focusing its efforts on addressing the issue of stablecoin yields. This has emerged as the organization's primary concern, especially as Congress aims to enact legislation on crypto market structure ahead of the upcoming midterms.

data.title: Hindustan Zinc & CIMIC Group to establish India's 1st Zinc Tailings Recycling Facility; Vedanta Group's contract with Sedgman & Leighton Asia.
data.description: In Udaipur, India, on January 23, 2026, Hindustan Zinc Limited, a company under the Vedanta Group and the top global zinc producer, revealed that it has granted contracts to CIMIC Group subsidiaries Sedgman and Leighton Asia. The contracts are for the construction of India's initial...

data.title: Antlara Dental in Turkey unveils in-house lab for dental restorations, merging digital design with expert hand finishing.
data.description: A new in-house laboratory service has been introduced by Antlara Dental in Antalya, Turkey, as of January 23, 2026. The service aims to streamline the process of creating and fitting dental restorations. By combining modern digital design and production techniques with expert hand finishing, the laboratory ensures a seamless workflow for patients. This advancement is set to enhance the overall efficiency and quality of dental services offered by Antlara Dental.

data.title: Analysts project SUI to reach $2.00-$2.42 in Feb, currently trading at $1.51 with potential for a rebound.
data.description: Trading at $1.51, SUI is currently experiencing bearish momentum. Despite this, analysts are maintaining February targets ranging from $2.00 to $2.42. Technical analysis indicates oversold conditions that could potentially initiate a rebound.

data.title: PwC says crypto adoption varies globally, facing diverse challenges in different regions.
data.description: According to a report by PricewaterhouseCoopers, the cryptocurrency sector is operating within a fragmented global environment, encountering diverse obstacles in various markets.

data.title: Bitcoin miners prepare for large ice storm approaching southern US. In 2022, Texas miners reduced activity during a winter storm.
data.description: During a major winter storm in 2022, Bitcoin miners in Texas reduced their activities to alleviate strain on the grid. This move demonstrates the industry's willingness to adapt and cooperate with energy needs during times of crisis. It showcased responsible action, as miners understand the importance of grid stability during critical periods. This proactive measure also emphasized the sector's commitment to sustainable energy practices and supporting the overall energy infrastructure. The decision underscores the significance of industry players being mindful of their energy consumption and its impact on the broader community.

data.title: Vallourec to exhibit Delphy, their hydrogen storage solution, at Hyvolution 2026.
data.description: A recent report revealed that the global charity organization donated over $5 million to fund various projects around the world. The donation will benefit over 10,000 individuals in need of healthcare, education, and clean water. The charity aims to improve the lives of disadvantaged communities and highlights the importance of tackling poverty and inequality. The donation is part of the organization's commitment to making a positive impact on society.

data.title: Wonderful and Anthropic partner to launch Agent Builder reducing time to production by 50% and early issues by 20%.
data.description: A new Agent Builder has been introduced, boasting a reduction in time to production by as much as half and a decrease in early production issues by 20%. In an effort to assist enterprises in managing large networks of agents, Wonderful has teamed up with the frontier AI lab Anthropic. The collaboration aims to establish a strong foundation for scaling operations. The announcement was made in Amsterdam on January 23, 2026.

data.title: Truecaller reveals Q4 2025 preliminary financial results; follows up on mid-Dec announcement; focus on ad revenue.
data.description: In Stockholm on January 23, 2026, Truecaller revealed its preliminary and unaudited financial results for Q4 2025. The company provided an update following a mid-December estimate of ad revenue for the period.

data.title: ARB price may hit $0.25-$0.28 by Feb 2026. Short-term target: $0.19-$0.21; critical support at $0.17.
data.description: Estimated ARB price predictions have been outlined as follows: In the short term (1 week), the target range is expected to be between $0.19 and $0.21. Looking at the medium term (1 month), forecasts suggest a range of $0.25 to $0.28. An optimistic breakout point stands at $0.19, while crucial support is identified at $0.17. These figures provide a snapshot of potential price movements for ARB in the coming weeks, indicating both target ranges and important levels to watch for.

data.title: Nasdaq seeks to eliminate position limits on Bitcoin, Ether ETF options, citing unequal treatment in derivatives markets.
data.description: A recent filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission has removed contract caps on crypto Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) options. Nasdaq has advocated for this change, stating that it aims to rectify disparities in derivatives markets. This move has significant implications for investors and traders participating in the crypto market, as it can potentially lead to increased trading volume and liquidity. By eliminating the contract caps, the potential for greater flexibility and opportunities in the crypto ETF options market is enhanced, offering a more level playing field for market participants.

data.title: OP Price Prediction: Expected to reach $0.37 by February with potential rise to $0.42; key resistance break crucial for uptrend.
data.description: Analysts are optimistic about Optimism (OP) seeing a potential increase to the $0.37-$0.42 range in the next 4 weeks, despite its current consolidation at $0.32. They have identified bullish momentum in the stock, but emphasize that a key resistance break is necessary to confirm further upside potential.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares expected to report Q4 earnings of $43.91/share, down from $45.1/share last year, with revenue of $2.29 billion, up from $2.23 billion. Company's stock value rose 1% to close at $2,203.53. Executive VP & Chief Risk Officer announced retirement.
data.description: First Citizens BancShares, Inc. (NASDAQ: FCNCA) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Friday, Jan. 23 before the market opens. Analysts predict the Jacksonville, Florida-based company will post earnings of $43.91 per share for the quarter, a decrease from $45.10 per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus among analysts is that First Citizens BancShares will report quarterly revenue of $2.29 billion, up from $2.23 billion in the previous year. Lorie K. Rupp, the executive vice president and chief risk officer, announced her retirement on Jan. 14. The stock of First Citizens BancShares closed at $2,203.53 on Thursday, marking a 1% increase. More details on analyst ratings can be found on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.description: The stock price of APT is currently at $1.58, with analysts predicting a short-term target price range of $2.00-$2.10 and a medium-term target range of $2.10-$2.43. Technical analysis indicates a neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) of 39.83. If resistance levels are surpassed, there is a potential for over 30% upside in value.
